qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Windows backslash traversal in TemplateLookup permits template-root escape
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets a Windows-only backslash separator bypass in 1.3.11, while the advisory describes the earlier double-slash URI prefix bypass caused by inconsistent leading-slash stripping before 1.3.11.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's double-slash URI prefix traversal root cause.
evidence: GHSA-v92g-xgxw-vvmm describes `//../../../...` bypassing the traversal guard because `TemplateLookup.get_template()` removed all leading `/` characters while `Template.__init__` removed only one before `os.path.normpath()`.

The qualifying finding instead describes a Windows-only bypass where backslashes survive `posixpath` handling in `TemplateLookup.get_template()` and are later interpreted as separators by Windows path handling.

The advisory patch `e05ac61989a7fb9dd7dcde6cfd72dc48328719a3` changes `Template.__init__` to `lstrip("/")` and adds `//` and `///` regression tests, while the finding's cited later patch `72e10c573ca0fbcbddd4455abca8ce92a61780d7` separately normalizes `\\` to `/` in both code paths as an incomplete fix follow-up.
